---
title: "ICE³ — Multi-Century Global Glacier Evolution"
status: active
start: 2024
---


# ICE³: Modelling the Global Multi-Century Evolution of Glacier Ice in 3D

**Status:** Active (2024–2025)  
**Institution:** Vrije Universiteit Brussel  
**Supervisor:** Harry Zekollari

## Summary

The ICE³ project aims to model the global multi-century evolution of glacier ice in three dimensions. Understanding the long-term response of glaciers to climate change is critical for projecting sea-level rise, water availability, and geohazards over timescales beyond the typical 21st-century projections.

## Methods

- 3D ice dynamics modelling using the Parallel Ice Sheet Model (PISM) and Open Global Glacier Model (OGGM)
- CMIP6 climate forcing ensembles
- Multi-century simulations extending to 2300
- Uncertainty quantification through parameter sensitivity analysis

## Datasets

- Randolph Glacier Inventory
- CMIP6 GCM outputs
- Reference climatologies (ERA5, WorldClim)

## Related Publications

- Zekollari et al. (2024), *The Cryosphere* — global glacier evolution under CMIP6
- Van Tricht et al. (2026), *Nature Climate Change* — peak glacier extinction

## Code

- [GitHub](https://github.com/rodaguayo)
